cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking which statement is true about Wu Jinyou based on the provided article. Let me go through the text again to find the relevant information. First, looking for mentions of Wu Jinyou. The article says, "Wu Jinyou, head of the Polar Research Institute of China, and the group's leader, said, 'I hope the students have not only a field trip to the Yellow River station, but are also touched by the devotion and spirit of the Chinese researchers here. I also expect in the future, some of them will choose to devote themselves to polar research and maybe even work with us.'" So, from that quote, Wu Jinyou is the head of the Polar Research Institute of China and the leader of the group. His statements express that he hopes the students are inspired by the researchers' devotion and spirit (option C mentions this), and he expects some might become polar researchers. Now, looking at the answer choices: (A) He is a graduate from Dalian Marinetime University. The article mentions Zheng Li is a student from Dalian Marinetime University, but there's no mention of Wu Jinyou's education. So A is not supported. (B) He is the president of Dalian Marinetime University. Again, no mention of that. He's the head of the Polar Research Institute, not linked to the university in the text. B is incorrect. (C) He thinks highly of the devotion and spirit of the Chinese researchers. The quote says he hopes the students are "touched by the devotion and spirit of the Chinese researchers here," which implies he values or thinks highly of them. So C is likely correct.
